title: Multiple systemverilog files
body:
I have a set of four systemverilog files,  two are types files and two contain systemverilog logic that I will eventually synthesize.  The file list is **sd_types,.svh sdspi_types.svh, sdspi.sv and sd.sv.**  The top module is sd.sv.

The two sv files contain **import** statements but no includes.

with verilator the following command works (parses correctly and generates c++.) 
    **verilator -cc  --top-module sd  sdspi_types.svh sd_types.svh sdspi.sv sd.sv** 
similarly verible-verilog works correctly ( with lots of lint warnings, but no errors)
     **verible-verilog-lint sdspi_types.svh sd_types.svh sd.sv**

I am able to successfully convert these systemverilog files to verilog with: 
     **sv2v    sd_types.svh sdspi_types.svh sdspi.sv sd.sv > sd.v**

I would like to be able to simulate this logic with iverilog but have been unable to get it to accept the list of files without a syntax error! I expect that it is simply me misunderstanding how to do this...  of course there would be a test bed file as well but until iverilog accepts the file list there is no point...

iverilog -g2012 ....
